---
title: "WR696_objective_2"
author: "Lynette Schwanger"
date: "2026-04-11"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(broom)
```

```{r}
pre_q <- read_csv("data/LE_Qseries.csv")

head(pre_q)
```

```{r}
pre_q <- pre_q |>
  filter(date_time >= as_datetime("2024-07-17 00:00:00"), date_time <= as_datetime("2025-07-21 23:59:59")) |>
  mutate(date = as_date(date_time))
```

```{r}
pre_q_long <- pre_q |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(ds_stage_ft, ds_q_cfs, us_stage_ft, us_q_cfs), names_to = c("location", ".value"), names_pattern = "(ds|us)_(.*)") |>
  mutate(location = if_else(location == "ds", "Downstream", "Upstream"))
```

```{r}
fits <- pre_q_long |>
  filter(stage_ft > 0, q_cfs > 0) |>  
  group_by(location) |>
  summarise(model   = list(lm(log(q_cfs) ~ log(stage_ft), data = cur_data())), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(a = map_dbl(model, ~ exp(coef(.x)[1])),   
    b = map_dbl(model, ~ coef(.x)[2]),         
    r2 = map_dbl(model, ~ summary(.x)$r.squared),
    label = paste0("Q = ", round(a, 3), " · h^", round(b, 3),
                   "\nR² = ", round(r2, 3)))

fits |> select(location, a, b, r2)
```

```{r}
fits_nls <- pre_q_long |>
  filter(stage_ft > 0, q_cfs > 0) |>
  group_by(location) |>
  summarise(
    model   = list(nls(q_cfs ~ a * stage_ft^b,
                       data = cur_data(),
                       start = list(a = 1, b = 1))),
    ss_tot  = sum((q_cfs - mean(q_cfs))^2),   # compute inside summarise
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    a  = map_dbl(model, ~ coef(.x)["a"]),
    b  = map_dbl(model, ~ coef(.x)["b"]),
    r2 = map2_dbl(model, ss_tot,
                  ~ 1 - sum(residuals(.x)^2) / .y)
  )

fits_nls |> select(location, a, b, r2)
```

```{r}
equation_labels <- fits_nls |>
  mutate(label = paste0("Q = ", round(a, 2), " · h^", round(b, 2),
                        "\nR² = ", round(r2, 3)), 
         location = factor(location, levels = c("Upstream", "Downstream")))
```


```{r}
#gah need to fix so upstream is first
ggplot(pre_q_long |> filter(stage_ft > 0, q_cfs > 0),
       aes(x = stage_ft, y = q_cfs)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = "Observed"), alpha = 1, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color = "Fitted Rating Curve"), method = "nls",
              method.args = list(formula = y ~ a * x^b,
                                 start = list(a = 1, b = 1)),
              se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Observed" = "steelblue2", "Fitted Rating Curve" = "navy")) +
  facet_wrap(~ location) +
  labs(title = "Stage-Discharge Rating Curves at Lower Elkhorn Pre-Restoration",
    subtitle = "July 2024 to July 2025",
    x  = "Stage (ft)",
    y = "Discharge (cfs)", 
    color = NULL) +
  geom_text(data = equation_labels,
          aes(x = Inf, y = -Inf, label = label),
          hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.5,
          size = 3.5, fontface = "italic",
          inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = c(0.10, .85), legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "grey80"))
```

#curious how rating curve relates to seasons
```{r}
pre_q_long_seasons <- pre_q_long |>
  mutate(season = case_when(
    month(date_time) %in% c(12, 1, 2) ~ "Winter",
    month(date_time) %in% c(3, 4, 5)  ~ "Spring",
    month(date_time) %in% c(6, 7, 8)  ~ "Summer",
    month(date_time) %in% c(9, 10, 11) ~ "Fall"))
```

```{r}
#oh but I want upstream plot on the left
pre_q_long_seasons <- pre_q_long_seasons |>
  mutate(location = factor(location, levels = c("Upstream", "Downstream")))

```


```{r}
#color observed by season
#oh duh, no winter data
#oh and I want the upstream plot on the left, fixed equation labels

ggplot(pre_q_long_seasons |> 
         filter(stage_ft > 0, q_cfs > 0) |>
         mutate(location = factor(location, levels = c("Upstream", "Downstream"))), 
       aes(x = stage_ft, y = q_cfs)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = season), alpha = 0.8, size = 1) +
scale_color_manual(values = c("Spring" = "forestgreen",
                               "Summer" = "goldenrod",
                               "Fall"   = "sienna"),
                   name = "Season") +
  geom_smooth(aes(linetype = "Fitted Rating Curve"),
            method = "nls",
            method.args = list(formula = y ~ a * x^b,
                               start = list(a = 1, b = 1)),
            se = FALSE, color = "navy", linewidth = 1) +
scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Fitted Rating Curve" = "solid"),
                      name = NULL) +
  facet_wrap(~ location) +
  labs(title = "Stage-Discharge Rating Curves at Lower Elkhorn Pre-Restoration",
    subtitle = "July 2024 to July 2025",
    x  = "Stage (ft)",
    y = "Discharge (cfs)", 
    color = NULL) +
  geom_text(data = equation_labels,
          aes(x = Inf, y = -Inf, label = label),
          hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.5,
          size = 3.5, fontface = "italic",
          inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = c(0.10, .85), legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "grey80"))

```

```{r}
#why those yellow summer points off to the right of the rating curve?
outliers <- pre_q_long_seasons |>
  filter(location == "Downstream",
         season == "Summer",
         stage_ft > 1.3) |>
  distinct(date) |>
  arrange(date)

print(outliers)
#maybe a storm on 8-9-2024? 
#and then something happened from 6-1-2024 to 6-9-2024, maybe peak snowmelt?
```
